title: How governments and organizations are leveraging Google’s AI breakthroughs for crisis resilience
gist: Google details how its AI forecasting, alerting and satellite-imagery products are being deployed with the UN, governments and NGOs for disaster resilience — from WeatherNext predicting Hurricane Melissa's landfall five days out and Flood Hub covering 2 billion people, to the new FireSat wildfire constellation, Android earthquake alerts, and DISHA/UNOSAT AI damage assessment of hundreds of thousands of buildings.

insights.claim: The US National Hurricane Center used Google's WeatherNext model during the 2025 hurricane season, which predicted Hurricane Melissa's historic Jamaican landfall five days in advance.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: Adoption of a Google AI weather model by the official US hurricane authority signals AI forecasting displacing legacy numerical models.
insights.quote: During the 2025 hurricane season, the U.S. National Hurricane Center used Google's WeatherNext model. It predicted Hurricane Melissa's historic Jamaican landfall five days in advance

insights.claim: Google's flood forecasts are available on Flood Hub covering 2 billion people across more than 150 countries in areas at risk for significant flood events.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: Two-billion-person reach establishes Google as default infrastructure for flood forecasting, extending its platform moat into public-safety.
insights.quote: Our forecasts are available on Flood Hub, covering 2 billion people across more than 150 countries, in areas at risk for significant flood events.

insights.claim: A Google pilot with the WMO and national hydrological agencies in Czechia, Nigeria, Uruguay and Vietnam found that incorporating local streamflow data into global AI models significantly improves forecasts in ungauged areas.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: The finding that global AI plus local data beats either alone is a blueprint favoring hybrid public-private forecasting deployments.
insights.quote: We found that incorporating local streamflow data into global AI models significantly improves forecasts in ungauged areas.

insights.claim: Google open-sourced its Groundsource dataset for urban flash floods and its hydrology modeling framework, and tested the framework with the Czech Hydrometeorological Institute which built an adapter for standard workflows.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: Open-sourcing hydrology tooling seeds ecosystem adoption while keeping Google's models at the center of national forecasting workflows.
insights.quote: we recently open-sourced our Groundsource dataset for urban flash floods, and our hydrology modeling framework

insights.claim: UN OCHA launched a Floods Anticipatory Action Programme in Nigeria's Adamawa state using Google's river flood forecasts to trigger early interventions like shelter preparation when high flood risk is indicated.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: Forecast-triggered pre-disbursement of aid is a model shift toward anticipatory humanitarian finance built on AI signals.
insights.quote: UN OCHA launched a Floods Anticipatory Action Programme using Google's river flood forecasts

insights.claim: NGO GiveDirectly used Google's forecasts in Nigeria's Kogi State to deliver cash transfers before flooding, enabling families to evacuate and buy protective equipment like sandbags.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: Pre-flood cash transfers demonstrate a scalable, forecast-driven aid model with measurable resilience benefits.
insights.quote: using Google's forecasts to deliver cash transfers before flooding. This enabled families to evacuate safely and purchase equipment like sandbags to protect their property.

insights.claim: Google, the Earth Fire Alliance and Muon Space developed the purpose-built FireSat constellation to detect wildfires more quickly before they spread, and three new FireSat satellites launched from Vandenberg Space Force Base.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: A dedicated wildfire-detection satellite constellation is a new space-based asset class with fire-agency and insurance customers.
insights.quote: we developed the purpose-built FireSat constellation, which aims to provide an unprecedented, wildfire dataset and help fire agencies detect wildfires more quickly before they spread

insights.claim: Google's Android Earthquake alerting system uses a network of Android phones as mini-seismometers, and alerted millions of users outside the epicenter seconds before shaking began when earthquakes hit Venezuela.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: Turning the Android install base into a seismic sensor network is a distribution moat competitors cannot easily replicate.
insights.quote: leveraging a network of Android phones as mini-seismometers, alerted millions of users outside the epicenter enabling them to take cover seconds before the shaking began
insights.locator: Providing life-saving alerts in times of crisis

insights.claim: Google helped connect people with crisis information over 10 million times per day on average in 2025, and its Public Alerts feature data from authorities in over 90 countries via Common Alerting Protocol feeds.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: Ten-million-daily crisis touchpoints deepen Google's role as critical public-safety infrastructure across Search, Maps and Android.
insights.quote: In 2025 alone, Google helped connect people with crisis information over 10 million times per day, on average.
insights.locator: Providing life-saving alerts in times of crisis

insights.claim: The DISHA damage-assessment workflow, built with Google and implemented with the UN Satellite Centre UNOSAT, uses Open Buildings and Building Damage Assessment models on satellite imagery and has been deployed 11 times, saving specialists weeks of work per activation.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: AI cutting damage-assessment time from weeks to short timeframes is a concrete productivity case for geospatial AI in humanitarian response.
insights.quote: It enables high-precision analysis of hundreds of thousands of buildings in very short timeframes, saving UNOSAT specialists weeks of work per activation.
insights.locator: Using satellite imagery to accelerate disaster response

insights.claim: When Hurricane Melissa devastated Jamaica in October 2025, Google's AI analysis assigned preliminary damage scores to over 385,000 buildings to inform recovery.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: Scoring 385,000 buildings post-hurricane demonstrates operational scale that recovery-funding and reinsurance workflows can leverage.
insights.quote: this AI-based analysis assigned preliminary damage scores to over 385,000 buildings to inform recovery efforts
insights.locator: Using satellite imagery to accelerate disaster response

insights.claim: Google has consolidated its climate and geospatial models into the Google Earth AI collection to provide planetary intelligence for disaster response and planetary monitoring for businesses and organizations.
insights.speaker: Yossi Matias
insights.so_what: Packaging geospatial models into a commercial collection signals Google monetizing planetary intelligence beyond humanitarian use.
insights.quote: We've brought together our climate and geospatial models in the Google Earth AI collection of models and datasets.
insights.locator: Using satellite imagery to accelerate disaster response
